LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS DEBBIE RAMIREZ, ROBERT BURKE AND BRENDAN BURKE'S NOTICE OF INTENT TO MOVE FOR NEW TRIAL AFTER GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION FOR A NEW TRIAL IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT EFFECTIVELY DEMONSTRATED WHY THE EVIDENCE THEY SEEK TO INTRODUCE WAS NOT PRODUCED BEFORE. IT IS UNDENIABLE FROM PLAINTIFFS' OPPOSITION THAT PLAINTIFFS UNDERSTOOD THAT THE PROPENSITY FOR CRIME IN THE PARKING LOT LOCATION WAS AN ISSUE TO BE ADDRESSED AND PLAINTIFFS PRESENTED EVIDENCE ON THIS ISSUE. THE DECLARATIONS SUBMITTED BY THE POLICE OFFICERS IN THE MOTION FOR A NEW TRIAL EXPLAIN WHY THEY WOULD NOT HAVE LEFT THE YOUTH IN THE PARKING LOT. IT IS NOT THE COURT'S CONCLUSION THAT IT WAS A PRUDENT DECISION BY THE CHP OFFICERS TO LEAVE THE YOUNG MEN IN THE PARKING LOT. IN DECIDING THIS CASE, THE QUESTION IS ONE OF THE FORESEEABILITY OF HARM THAT RESULTED FROM THE OFFICERS' ACTIONS. IT IS THE COURT'S CONCLUSION THAT IT IS SIMPLY NOT FORESEEABLE THAT A MAN WITH A GUN WOULD SHOT THE YOUNG MEN IN THIS PARKING LOT AT 3:00 A.M., WHETHER THE ASSAILANT BE A STRANGER OR, AS IN THIS CASE, A MAN WITH A GUN WHO HAD A MOTIVE TO KILL ONE OR BOTH OF THE YOUNG MEN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Notice Of Intent To Move For New Trial After Granting Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-30-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS DEBBIE RAMIREZ, ROBERT BURKE AND BRENDAN BURKE'S Notice Of Intent To Move For New Trial After Granting Summary Judgment.
APPEARANCES: SHERYL LAND, ESQUIRE (LAW OFFICES OF MURRAY & ASSOCIATES, 415.673.0555) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. THERE ARE NO OTHER APPEARANCES.
RULING: COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING:
The motion for a new trial is denied. Plaintiffs have not effectively demonstrated why the evidence they seek to introduce was not produced before. It is undeniable from plaintiffs' opposition that plaintiffs understood that the propensity for crime in the parking lot location was an issue to be addressed and plaintiffs presented evidence on this issue. The declarations submitted by the police officers in the motion for a new trial explain why they would not have left the youth in the parking lot. It is not the Court's conclusion that it was a prudent decision by the CHP officers to leave the young men in the parking lot. In deciding this case, the question is one of the foresee ability of harm that resulted from the officers' actions. It is the Court's conclusion that it is simply not foreseeable that a man with a gun would shot the young men in this parking lot at 3:00 a.m., whether the assailant be a stranger or, as in this case, a man with a gun who had a motive to kill one or both of the young men. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
